channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence
item.description: "I think we think of A.I. as primarily a bunch of the software geeks who are out there coding somewhere, but there's real hardware involved," says Neil Chilson. The post A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Another mini PC with a Ryzen AI 9 HX CPU has emerged, and not only does it support four 4K monitors, it can also take up to 96GB memory
item.description: Acemagic F3A mini PC combines cutting-edge AMD Ryzen AI technology with impressive performance capabilities for demanding users.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sen. Josh Hawley Announces Legislation to Empower Parents to Sue Social Media Companies
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley introduces legislation to allow parents to sue social media companies over alleged harms to children and teens, citing lack of accountability and liability shield under Section 230
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Did extreme overclockers inspire Microsoft to develop closed loop liquid cooling for data centers? I'd like to think so
item.description: Microsoft is switching to closed-loop liquid cooling, as used by PC overclockers, for its data centers to eliminate water wastage.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Rumor Points to Late February Press Event for AMD RX 9070 GPUs - A Big Reveal Ahead of March On-Sale Date?
item.description: Rumors suggest AMD will hold a press conference for the Radeon RX 9000 series, including the RX 9070 GPU, at the end of February. This could be a big reveal with pricing details ahead of the scheduled March on-sale date.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sony needs a bit of gentle encouragement with the PlayStation 5, and this rumored Steam console from Valve might be the perfect answer for that
item.description: If the Steam Deck was anything to go by, Valve has a very good shot at competing in the home gaming market - if rumors of a Steam console are true.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Nothing Phone 3a could borrow the iPhone 16’s best feature – and I think it’s a great idea
item.description: Nothing has released another teaser for the upcoming Nothing Phone 3a, its next midrange phone, with strong hints that the new phone will come with a shutter button similar to the iPhone 16’s Camera Control button.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Europe’s DMA gives another big boost to iOS platform decay
item.description: One of a handful of “independent” iOS app stores in Europe has begun the distribution of a porn app, advertising it as “Apple Approved.” The app isn’t approved by Apple, and the biggest app available on the AltStore is Epic’s child-focused Fortnite game.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta sets Oculus Quest headset shelf-life at six years, but there's still hope that the Meta Quest 2 will survive past 2026
item.description: Meta has ended all software updates to the Oculus Quest, which suggests the Quest 2 could enter stasis in 2026.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin mimics the moon's gravity for NASA experiments during spaceflight
item.description: Jeff Bezos' rocket company has given NASA a brief taste of the moon's gravity without straying too far from home.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Phishing campaign targets prominent X users, accounts at risk
item.description: A phishing campaign is hijacking high profile X users and using their accounts for fraudulent activity.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta reveals what kinds of AI even it would think too risky to release
item.description: Meta wants to work with industry experts, governments, and others to stop AI from becoming a problem to society.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: This scrappy search upstart is getting thousands of people to give up Google
item.description: Search today sure ain’t what it used to be. On the one hand, you’ve got the escalating sense that Google’s once-reliable results are stuck in a downward spiral.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Everything new on Prime Video in February 2025
item.description: From rom-coms to movie classics, which will you pick to watch out of everything new on Prime Video in February 2025?
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: AMD patches microcode security holes after accidental early disclosure
item.description: AMD on Monday issued two patches for severe microcode security flaws, defects that AMD said “could lead to the loss of Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) protection.” The bugs were inadvertently revealed by a partner last week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, The Hill

item.title: Tariffs on Computer Chips: President Trump's Threats and Industry Consequences
item.description: President Donald Trump threatens tariffs on computer chips made in Taiwan, impacting major manufacturers like TSMC, Nvidia, and Apple. Experts warn of negative consequences for the tech industry and economies globally.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Linux Foundation’s L3AF brings zero-downtime updates to eBPF network management
item.description: A challenge for enterprises that operate large-scale network infrastructure has long been how to maintain performance while updating critical systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The DeepSeek lesson
item.description: During the past two weeks, DeepSeek unraveled Silicon Valley’s comfortable narrative about generative artificial intelligence by introducing dramatically more efficient ways to scale large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI investments face integration, compliance, and skills challenges: survey
item.description: Network engineers report that they see the current and future value in using artificial intelligence for network management and cybersecurity purposes, according to new survey results, but they also anticipate more barriers to successful adoption than their C-suite counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Industry Exchange Cyber 2025: Zimperium’s Kern Smith on why securing mobile devices doesn’t necessarily mean locking them down
item.description: With the right continuous monitoring of mobile activity and apps, agencies can avoid restrictive device policies, advises Zimperium’s solutions engineering VP.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Cybersecurity has had its ups and downs as we head into 2025
item.description: Last year was an eventful one on the cybersecurity front. But big cyber developments in the federal scape are expected in 2025. The post Cybersecurity has had its ups and downs as we head into 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
